steps.title: Recognize the standard form of a circle equation
steps.content: The equation $(x+2)^2 + (y+5)^2 = 169$ matches the standard form $(x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2 = r^2$. In this form, the number on the right side of the equation is $r^2$ (the radius squared), not the radius itself.

steps.title: Extract the radius from the equation
steps.content: From the equation, we have $r^2 = 169$. To find the radius, take the square root of both sides: $$r = \sqrt{169} = 13$$ The radius of the circle is 13.
